2603.28764.title: Geometry-aware similarity metrics for neural representations on Riemannian and statistical manifolds
2603.28764.paper_summary: Similarity measures are widely used to interpret the representational geometries used by neural networks to solve tasks. Yet, because existing methods compare the extrinsic geometry of representations in state space, rather than their intrinsic geometry, they may fail to capture subtle yet crucial distinctions between fundamentally different neural network solutions. Here, we introduce metric similarity analysis (MSA), a novel method which leverages tools from Riemannian geometry to compare the intrinsic geometry of neural representations under the manifold hypothesis. We show that MSA can be used to i) disentangle features of neural computations in deep networks with different learning regimes, ii) compare nonlinear dynamics, and iii) investigate diffusion models. Hence, we introduce a mathematically grounded and broadly applicable framework to understand the mechanisms behind neural computations by comparing their intrinsic geometries.
2603.28764.paper_summary_zh: 相似性度量被廣泛用於解釋神經網絡用來解決任務的表徵幾何。<lb/>然而，由於現有的方法比較的是狀態空間中表徵的外在幾何，而非其內在幾何，它們可能無法捕捉到根本不同的神經網絡解決方案之間微妙但關鍵的區別。<lb/>在此，我們介紹了度量相似性分析（MSA），這是一種新穎的方法，利用黎曼幾何的工具來比較神經表徵在流形假設下的內在幾何。<lb/>我們展示了MSA可以用於 i) 解開具有不同學習模式的深度網絡中的神經計算特徵， ii) 比較非線性動態，以及 iii) 研究擴散模型。<lb/>因此，我們引入了一個數學上有根據且廣泛適用的框架，以通過比較其內在幾何來理解神經計算背後的機制。
2603.28764.author: N Alex Cayco Gajic et.al.
2603.28764.authors: N Alex Cayco Gajic, Arthur Pellegrino
